- Pressing RIGHT moved the green box W from [30,18]-[32,20] to [30,21]-[32,23] (not from the cursor position)
- So direction keys don't move the green box at the cursor position; they move the green box to the LEFT of the cursor
Hmm, but after 9 RIGHT presses, the green box W reached the cursor position [30,30]-[32,32]. After that, pressing RIGHT didn't do anything (board_changed: false).
So maybe the mechanic is:
1. The cursor selects the leftmost green box in its row
2. Direction keys move the selected green box
3. When the selected green box reaches the cursor position, the cursor selects the next green box to the right
4. If there are no more green boxes to the right, direction keys don't do anything
But after clicking on the green box B, the cursor "selected" the green box B (which was at [30,42]-[32,44]). Then pressing UP + RIGHT moved the green box B to [27,45]-[29,47].
So the mechanic might be:
1. MOUSE clicks on a green box to select it
2. Direction keys move the selected green box
3. The cursor position doesn't matter for movement; it's just a visual indicator
But then why did the green box W move when I pressed RIGHT initially? Maybe the cursor automatically selects the leftmost green box when the level starts.
